production_note: agentic_K1 dual-LLM explorer: sealed receipt write; packet-only; no bulk writers; no identity stamps
knowledge_boundary: Only landing + prior + active packet content.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
flags = np.array([[1, 0], [1, 1], [0, 1]], dtype=np.uint8)
print(flags.dtype, flags.shape, flags.ndim)
exercises.justification_from_packet: T1-A: dtype fija el tipo homogéneo y shape/ndim validan el contrato del array; el starter ya trae uint8 (3,2) y se imprimen esos tres atributos.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
scores = np.linspace(0, 1, 5, dtype=np.float64)
print(scores.itemsize, scores.nbytes, scores.tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: linspace con dtype float64 da 5 puntos de 0 a 1; itemsize es 8 y nbytes=size*itemsize=40 según T1-A/T4-B.
Contrato fail-closed de T1-A: validar ndim y dtype al recibir un array; si no cuadra, ValueError en vez de rellenar en silencio.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
score = np.array([0.2, 0.8, 0.4, 0.9])
mask = score >= 0.5
print(np.where(mask)[0].tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: T1-B: máscara booleana score>=0.5 y np.where devuelve índices 0-based de quienes cumplen la condición.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
ids = np.array(["C001", "C002", "C003", "C004"])
scores = np.array([0.1, 0.9, 0.4, 0.7])
med = np.median(scores)
print(ids[scores < med].tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Filtrar con máscara scores < mediana es la forma idiomática de calidad de T1-B; ids[mask] selecciona C001 y C003.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
a = np.array([10, 20, 30, 40])
order = [2, 0, 3, 1]
print(a[order].tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Fancy index a[[i,...]] reordena sin loop (T1-B); order [2,0,3,1] produce [30,10,40,20].

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
M = np.array([[1., 0., 1.], [1., 1., 0.]])
por_col = np.round(M.mean(axis=0), 2).tolist()
por_fil = np.round(M.mean(axis=1), 2).tolist()
print(por_col, '|', por_fil)
exercises.justification_from_packet: T2-A: axis=0 colapsa filas (por columna) y axis=1 por fila; mean de flags da completitud.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
scores = np.array([[0.5, 0.5], [1.0, 0.0], [0.8, 0.6]])
print(np.round(scores.std(axis=0), 4).tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: std(scores, axis=0) mide dispersión por variable/columna (T2-A); ddof=0 por defecto en np.std.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
X = np.array([[1., 3.], [10., 20.], [2., 2.]])
Xc = X - X.mean(axis=1, keepdims=True)
print(np.round(Xc.mean(axis=1), 1).tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: keepdims=True preserva dimensiones para restar la media por fila y rebroadcast (T2-A); media post-normalizar ~0.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
M = np.zeros((2, 3))
w = np.array([1., 2., 3.])
print((M + w).tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Broadcast alinea por la derecha: (2,3)+(3,) repite pesos en cada fila (T2-B).

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
a = np.arange(4)
b = np.arange(3)
prod = a[:, None] * b
print(prod.shape, prod.tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: newaxis/None inserta eje de tamaño 1 para señales por pares (T2-B); a[:,None]*b da shape (4,3).
Si shapes no son iguales ni 1, broadcasting lanza ValueError (T2-B); se documenta como incompatible.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
raw = np.array([1, 2, 3])
vista = raw[:2]
vista[0] = 9
print(raw.tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Slice simple suele ser view; mutar vista muta el base (T3-A).

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
raw = np.array([1, 2, 3])
copia = raw[:2].copy()
copia[0] = 9
print(raw.tolist(), copia.tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: .copy() aísla transformaciones; raw queda intacto al mutar la copia (T3-A).

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
a = np.array([1.0, 2.0])
a.flags.writeable = False
try:
    a[0] = 3.0
except ValueError:
    print('blocked')
exercises.justification_from_packet: flags.writeable controla mutación; asignar a no escribible lanza ValueError (T3-A).

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
x = np.array([1.0, np.nan, 2.0, np.nan])
print(int(np.isnan(x).sum()))
exercises.justification_from_packet: np.isnan marca ausencia; sum de la máscara cuenta NaNs (T3-B).

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
x = np.array([1.0, np.nan, 3.0])
print(float(np.round(np.nanmean(x), 1)))
exercises.justification_from_packet: nanmean ignora NaN; NaN es ausencia no cero (T3-B), media de 1 y 3 = 2.0.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
x = np.array([1.0, np.inf, 2.0])
y = np.where(np.isinf(x), np.nan, x)
print(float(np.nansum(y)))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Reemplazar inf por nan y nansum es la política robusta de T3-B/demos.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
a = np.arange(1000, dtype=float)
b = a.copy()
s_loop = 0.0
for i in range(len(a)):
    s_loop += a[i] * b[i]
s_vec = float((a * b).sum())
print(abs(s_loop - s_vec) < 1e-6)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Loop y (a*b).sum deben ser equivalentes; se compara resultado no tiempo (T4-A).

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
a = np.arange(5, dtype=float)
print(float((a ** 2).sum()))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Suma de cuadrados vectorizada con ufuncs en C (T4-A); 0+1+4+9+16=30.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np, time
n = 10000
a = np.zeros(n)
b = np.ones(n)
t0 = time.perf_counter()
res = a + b
_ = time.perf_counter() - t0
print('timed', bool(np.isclose(res.mean(), 1.0)))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Benchmark honesto con time.perf_counter y correctness (mean=1.0) como en T4-A.

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
a = np.zeros(1000, dtype=np.float64)
print(a.nbytes, a.nbytes == 8000)
exercises.justification_from_packet: nbytes = itemsize*size; float64=8 bytes → 8000 (T4-B).

exercises.code:
import numpy as np
print(bool(np.allclose([1.0, 2.0], [1.0 + 1e-9, 2.0], atol=1e-8)))
exercises.justification_from_packet: allclose con atol compara floats con tolerancia (T4-B); diferencia 1e-9 < 1e-8.
assert_allclose falla con mensaje claro cuando no hay equivalencia dentro de atol (T4-B).

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: T1-A: dtype fija el tipo homogéneo de los elementos del ndarray (shape es dimensiones, ndim es rango).
T1-B: máscara a>umbral filtra/selecciona elementos que cumplen la condición (a[mask]).
T2-A: axis=0 reduce filas → agrega por columna en matriz 2D.
T3-A: slice simple suele ser view; mutarlo puede mutar el array base.
